children.label: getWorkflow
description: Get a workflow by ID and space ID
signature: (workflowId: string, spaceId: string) => Promise<{ id: string; name: string; enabled: boolean; tags: string[]; createdAt: Date; createdBy: string; lastUpdatedAt: Date; lastUpdatedBy: string; deleted_at: any; yaml: string; valid: any; description?: string | undefined; definition?: any; } | null>

children.label: isWorkflowEnabled
description: Check if a workflow is enabled by ID and space ID

children.label: areWorkflowsEnabled
description:
Bulk-check whether the given (workflowId, spaceId) pairs refer to enabled,
non-soft-deleted workflows. Runs a single `_search` fetching only the
`enabled` field across all requested ids.

The returned map is keyed by `${spaceId}:${workflowId}`. Missing docs and
soft-deleted docs (`deleted_at` present) resolve to `false`.
signature: (refs: { workflowId: string; spaceId: string; }[]) => Promise<Map<string, boolean>>

functions.label: buildBuiltInStepSchema
description:
Build a simplified step YAML schema from a BaseStepDefinition (built-in steps).

For flow-control steps (if, foreach), the `configSchema` fields (`condition`,
`steps`, `else`, `foreach`) become top-level step properties. Common optional
properties (`if`, `timeout`) are added for non-flow-control steps only.

functions.label: buildConnectorStepSchema
description:
Build a simplified step YAML schema from a ConnectorContractUnion.

Produces the complete object shape an AI agent would write in workflow YAML:
`name`, `type`, `with` (params), `connector-id`, config fields, and common
optional step properties (`if`, `timeout`).

This is a simplified, non-recursive variant of `generateStepSchemaForConnector`
(which builds the full recursive schema used for validation). It intentionally
omits `on-failure` recursion so the result can be safely converted to JSON Schema
without infinite references.

functions.label: buildElasticsearchRequest
description:
Builds an Elasticsearch request from connector definitions
This is shared between the execution engine and the YAML editor copy functionality

functions.label: buildFieldsZodValidator
description:
Builds a Zod validator for workflow fields (inputs or outputs) from a normalized JSON Schema
(object with properties, required, etc.). Handles required/optional, defaults, and $ref resolution.
Use after normalizeFieldsToJsonSchema when validating field payloads.
signature: (schema: { type: any; title?: string | undefined; description?: string | undefined; $ref?: string | undefined; properties?: Record<string,
> | undefined; additionalProperties?: boolean | undefined; required?: string[] | undefined; definitions?: Record<string,

> | undefined; additionalProperties?: boolean | undefined; required?: string[] | undefined; definitions?: Record<string,

functions.label: buildKibanaRequest
description:
Builds a Kibana HTTP request from connector definitions
This is shared between the execution engine and the YAML editor copy functionality
signature: (actionType: string, params: Record<string, unknown>, spaceId: string | undefined) =>

functions.label: buildOutputSummary
description:
Produce a one-line summary of an output schema, e.g.
`"object with: took (number), errors (boolean), items (array)"`.

functions.label: buildStepParamsSummary
description:
Build a compact list of top-level parameters from a Zod object schema.
Only walks one level deep -- nested object details are omitted.

functions.label: convertJsonSchemaToZod
description:
Converts a JSON Schema to a Zod schema using fromJSONSchema.
Used as fallback when fromJSONSchema returns undefined or for $ref placeholders.

functions.label: convertJsonSchemaToZodWithRefs
description:
Recursively converts a JSON Schema to a Zod schema, resolving $ref against the root schema.
Use when you have a root schema (e.g. workflow inputs/outputs) and property schemas that may reference it.
signature: , rootSchema: { type: any; title?: string | undefined; description?: string | undefined; $ref?: string | undefined; properties?: Record<string,
> | undefined; additionalProperties?: boolean | undefined; required?: string[] | undefined; definitions?: Record<string,

children.label: rootSchema
signature: { type: any; title?: string | undefined; description?: string | undefined; $ref?: string | undefined; properties?: Record<string,
> | undefined; additionalProperties?: boolean | undefined; required?: string[] | undefined; definitions?: Record<string,

functions.label: createWorkflowLiquidEngine
description:
Creates a LiquidJS engine configured for workflow templates.
Uses an in-memory filesystem, restricts tags to the supported set,
and enables ownPropertyOnly.

Callers can pass additional {@link LiquidOptions} (e.g. `strictFilters`)
which are merged with the enforced defaults.

functions.label: createWorkflowSystemIndex
description: Helper function to define workflow system indices.

functions.label: extractPropertyPathsFromKql
description:
Extracts property paths from a KQL (Kibana Query Language) query string.

This function parses a KQL query and extracts field names that appear before
colons in field:value expressions, ignoring quoted strings and handling
logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) and parentheses.

It also handles template expressions ({{ ... }}) by:
1. Replacing them with placeholders before KQL parsing
2. Extracting template variables separately
3. Combining both sets of results

children.label: kql
description: - The KQL query string to parse

returnComment: Array of unique property paths found in the query

functions.label: extractTemplateVariables
description:
Extracts template variables from a Liquid template string.

This function parses a Liquid template and returns an array of variable paths that are referenced
in the template. It handles various Liquid constructs including conditionals, loops, filters, and
more, while filtering out local variables (like loop iterators) and correctly handling array/object
access patterns.

**Key behaviors:**
- **Local variables are excluded**: Variables created by `for`, `assign`, `capture`, and `tablerow`
  tags are not included in the output since they're not external inputs to the template.
- **Literal array/object access is preserved**: When accessing arrays or objects with literal indices
  (numbers or quoted strings), the full path is returned.
- **Variable references in brackets are truncated**: When a variable is used as an index/key
  (e.g., `items[i]` where `i` is a variable), the path is truncated at that point since the full
  path cannot be statically determined.

children.label: template
description: - A Liquid template string to parse

returnComment: An array of unique variable paths referenced in the template

children.label: triggers
description: /** Registered custom trigger type ids for YAML schema validation (e.g. example.custom_trigger) */

children.label: loose

functions.label: getElasticsearchConnectors
description:
Elasticsearch Connector Generation

This system provides ConnectorContract[] for all Elasticsearch APIs
by using pre-generated definitions from Console's API specifications.

Benefits:
1. **Schema Validation**: Zod schemas for all ES API parameters
2. **Autocomplete**: Monaco YAML editor gets full autocomplete via JSON Schema
3. **Comprehensive Coverage**: 568 Elasticsearch APIs supported
4. **Browser Compatible**: No file system access required
5. **Lazy Loading**: Large generated files are only loaded when needed, reducing main bundle size

The generated connectors include:
- All Console API definitions converted to Zod schemas
- Path parameters extracted from patterns (e.g., {index}, {id})
- URL parameters with proper types (flags, enums, strings, numbers)
- Proper ConnectorContract format for workflow execution

To regenerate: run 'node scripts/generate_workflow_es_contracts.js' from kibana root

functions.label: getTriggerSchema
description:
Returns a trigger schema that includes built-in types plus optional registered trigger ids.
Used by the YAML editor so custom trigger types (e.g. example.custom_trigger) pass validation.
Custom triggers allow an `on.condition` clause for KQL filtering.

functions.label: getWorkflowExample
description: Look up a single catalog entry by id.

functions.label: getWorkflowExamples
description:
Get workflow example catalog entries, optionally filtered by category and/or search term.
Returns metadata only (no YAML content).
signature: (filter: { category?: string | undefined; search?: string | undefined; } | undefined) =>

functions.label: getWorkflowExamplesDir
description:
Absolute path to the bundled examples directory.
Works in both dev and production because it uses `__dirname`.
Server-side callers can combine this with `readFileSync` to load YAML content.

functions.label: isEventDrivenWorkflowTriggerSource
description:
Returns true when `triggeredBy` is a custom event trigger id (non-empty and not well-known).
This helper is the single source of truth for event-driven trigger-id classification.

functions.label: isWellKnownWorkflowTriggerSource
description:
Returns true when `triggeredBy` is one of the platform-defined execution sources.
Used to distinguish built-in triggers from event-driven trigger ids in telemetry and APM.

functions.label: parseJsPropertyAccess
description:
Parses a JavaScript property access path into its constituent parts.

This function breaks down property access strings like "a.b.c", "a[0].b",
or "a['key'].b[1]" into an array of individual property names or indices.

children.label: path
description: - The property access path string to parse (e.g., "user.profile['name']")

returnComment: An array of strings representing each property access part

functions.label: validateKqlAgainstSchema
description:
Validates a KQL string in two ways:
1. Ensures the string is valid KQL (parseable syntax).
2. Ensures every field referenced in the KQL is a property path allowed by the schema.

children.label: kql
description: - The KQL string to validate (e.g. a trigger condition like `event.severity == "high"`).

children.label: schema
description: - A Zod schema describing allowed properties (e.g. event payload schema).

children.label: options
description: - Optional settings; use fieldPrefix when KQL uses a root (e.g. "event." for "event.severity").

returnComment: : true } or { valid: false, error: string }.

children.label: documentation
description: Documentation URL for this API endpoint

children.label: stability
description: API stability level derived from the OpenAPI `x-state` field

children.label: deprecation
description: Deprecation metadata for this step type.

children.label: editorHandlers
signature: <unknown, Record<string, unknown>, Record<string, unknown>> | undefined> | undefined; input?: Record<string,
<unknown, Record<string, unknown>, Record<string, unknown>> | undefined> | undefined; } | undefined

interfaces.label: BaseStepDefinition
description:
Base interface for all step definitions across the workflows system.
Both built-in steps (if, foreach, wait, data.set) and registry-based steps
(connector steps, AI steps, etc.) extend this interface.

children.label: id
description:
Unique identifier for this step type.
Should follow a namespaced format (e.g., "ai.prompt", "data.set", "elasticsearch.search").

children.label: label
description:
User-facing label/title for this step type.
Displayed in the UI when selecting or viewing steps.

children.label: description
description: Human-readable description of what this step does.

children.label: category
description: Category grouping for this step type.

children.label: inputSchema
description:
Zod schema for validating step input (the `with` block in YAML).
The input type is automatically inferred from this schema.

children.label: outputSchema
description:
Zod schema for validating step output.
The output type is automatically inferred from this schema.

children.label: configSchema
description:
Zod schema for validating step config properties.
Defines config properties that appear at the step level (outside the `with` block).
Example: `connector-id` for connector steps, `condition` for if steps.

children.label: documentation
description: Documentation for the step, including details and examples.

children.label: stability
description:
API stability level for this step (e.g. 'tech_preview', 'beta').
Omit for stable/GA steps.

children.label: deprecation
description:
Deprecation metadata for this step type.
Deprecated steps remain valid for existing workflows, but should not be
suggested for new workflows.

children.label: actionTypeId
description: Action type ID from Kibana actions plugin

children.label: instances
description: Available connector instances

children.label: enabled
description: Whether this connector type is enabled

children.label: isSystemActionType
description: Whether this is a system action type

children.label: stepId
description: If specified, the only this step and its children will be executed

children.label: error
signature: { type: string; message: string; details?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; } | null

children.label: stepExecutionIds
description: IDs of all step executions, enables O(1) mget lookup instead of search

children.label: metadata
description: Caller-supplied execution metadata, separate from workflow inputs

children.label: dispatchEventId
description: Trigger dispatch id from event-driven scheduling (`context.metadata.eventId`), when set

children.label: scopeStack
description: Current step's stack frames.

children.label: isTestRun
description: Whether this step execution belongs to a test run of the workflow.

children.label: topologicalIndex
description: Topological index of step in workflow graph.

children.label: globalExecutionIndex
description: Overall execution index in the entire workflow.

children.label: stepExecutionIndex
description:
Execution index within specific stepId.
There might be several instances of the same stepId if it's inside loops, retries, etc.

children.label: error
signature: { type: string; message: string; details?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; } | undefined

children.label: state
description: Specific step execution instance state. Used by loops, retries, etc to track execution context.

children.label: includeMetadata
description: When true, each entry includes `description` (from Zod `.describe()`) and `displayType`

children.label: methods
description: HTTP method(s) for this API endpoint

children.label: patterns
description: URL pattern(s) for this API endpoint

children.label: parameterTypes
description: Parameter type metadata for proper request building
signature: { headerParams: string[]; pathParams: string[]; urlParams: string[]; bodyParams: string[]; }

children.label: dependsOnValues
description:
Dot paths (e.g. `config.proxy.ssl`, `input.owner`) whose values are passed in `context.values`
and included in the selection cache key. If omitted or empty, `context.values` is `{ config: {}, input: {} }`.

children.label: search
description:
Search for options matching the input query.
Used by autocomplete dropdowns when the user types.

children.label: resolve
description:
Resolve an entity by its value.
Used when loading existing values or when a value is pasted.
Returns null if the entity is not found.

children.label: getDetails
description:
Get detailed information for the current value.
Used for decoration and metadata display in the editor.
The option parameter is the resolved entity from `resolve`, if available.

children.label: action
signature: (stepInputs?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined) => Promise<void | Record<string, unknown>>

children.label: bulkBody
description: Bulk body for elasticsearch.bulk step

children.label: stepType
description: The step type ID (e.g., "onechat.runAgent")

children.label: scope
description: The property path ("config" or "input")

children.label: propertyKey
description: The property key (e.g., "agent_id")

children.label: values
description: Sibling values of the current step, keyed by scope (only paths listed in `dependsOnValues` are populated).

children.label: message
description: Message to display (e.g., "✓ Agent connected" or "Agent not found")

children.label: links
description: Links to related actions (e.g., "Edit agent", "Create agent")

children.label: value
description: The value that will be stored in the YAML

children.label: label
description: The label displayed in the UI

children.label: description
description: Description shown in completion popup or tooltips (optional)

children.label: documentation
description: Extended documentation shown in side panel (optional)

children.label: stepId
description: Step that created this frame

children.label: nestedScopes
description: Scope entries within this frame

interfaces.label: StepDocumentation
description: Documentation information for a workflow step.

children.label: details
description: Detailed description with usage examples (markdown supported)

children.label: url
description: External documentation URL

children.label: examples
description: Usage examples in YAML format

interfaces.label: StepParamSummary
description: Compact representation of a single step parameter for the AI agent.

children.label: prefix
description: Prefix to match against step type (e.g., 'inference.' matches 'inference.completion')

children.label: selection
description:
Entity selection configuration for the property.
Provides a unified interface for search, resolution, and decoration of entity references.

children.label: connectorIdSelection
description:
Connector ID selection configuration for the property.
Used to resolve connector IDs for custom steps.

**Note**: This handler is currently only supported for the `connector-id` property in the config schema.

interfaces.label: StepSelectionValues
description:
Structured values of the current step, split by scope.

Built from scalar leaf properties in the YAML step definition.
Intermediate map nodes that have no scalar value are **not** represented;
a missing key means "not yet defined in the YAML", not "empty object".

children.label: config
description: Root-level step properties (everything outside the `with` block).
signature: { [P in keyof TConfig]?: (TConfig[P] extends NonAny[] ? TConfig[P] : TConfig[P] extends readonly NonAny[] ? TConfig[P] : TConfig[P] extends (infer U)[] ?
<V>> : TConfig[P] extends Map<infer K, infer V> ? Map<K,

children.label: input
description: Properties nested under the `with` block.
signature: { [P in keyof TInput]?: (TInput[P] extends NonAny[] ? TInput[P] : TInput[P] extends readonly NonAny[] ? TInput[P] : TInput[P] extends (infer U)[] ?
<V>> : TInput[P] extends Map<infer K, infer V> ? Map<K,

children.label: fieldPrefix
description:
Prefix for schema-derived field paths (e.g. "event" for trigger conditions using event.severity).
When set, allowed paths are prefix, prefix.path1, prefix.path2, ...
When omitted, allowed paths are the schema paths as-is.

children.label: filename
description: Filename relative to the examples directory (e.g. "basic.yml")

children.label: error
signature: { type: string; message: string; details?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; } | null

children.label: stepId
description: If specified, only this step and its children were executed

interfaces.label: WorkflowExecutionEngineModel
signature: extends Pick<{ id: string; name: string; enabled: boolean; tags: string[]; createdAt: Date; createdBy: string; lastUpdatedAt: Date; lastUpdatedBy: string; deleted_at: any; yaml: string; valid: any; description?: string | undefined; definition?: any; }, "id" | "name" | "enabled" | "definition" | "yaml">

interfaces.label: WorkflowExecutionEventDispatchMetadata
description:
Optional metadata passed when scheduling or executing a workflow from the
event-driven trigger path. Persisted on the execution document (see execution
engine `createAndPersistWorkflowExecution`) for latency and analytics.

children.label: eventDispatchTimestamp
description: ISO-8601 string or epoch ms when the emitting service handled the trigger event

children.label: eventTriggerId
description: Registered trigger id for the emission (e.g. cases.caseCreated)

children.label: eventId
description: UUID of the emission

misc.label: builtInStepDefinitions
description:
Built-in step definitions derived from the Zod schemas in schema.ts.
Each definition decomposes into inputSchema (the `with` block),
configSchema (step-level props like condition/steps/else/foreach),
and outputSchema.

misc.label: BulkCreateWorkflowsCommand

misc.label: BulkScheduleWorkflowResult

Per-item result of a bulk workflow schedule call. The array is order- and
length-preserving with respect to the input items: `results[i]` corresponds
to `items[i]`.
signature: ({ status: "scheduled"; workflowExecutionId: string; } | { status: "error"; error: { message: string; }; })[]

misc.label: DEPRECATED_STEP_PREFIX_METADATA
description:
Prefix-based deprecation for step types. Any step type starting with one of these
prefixes is treated as deprecated. This avoids enumerating every sub-action when an
entire connector family is superseded by a purpose-built step.

misc.label: StepWithOnFailure

misc.label: WellKnownWorkflowTriggerSource
description:
Values persisted on workflow execution `triggeredBy` for built-in trigger paths.
'workflow-step' is used for sub-workflows.
Any other string is treated as an event-driven trigger id (e.g. `cases.caseCreated`).

misc.label: WORKFLOW_EXAMPLES
description:
Catalog of bundled workflow examples. Metadata only -- YAML content is loaded
at runtime on the server via `loadWorkflowExampleContent()`.

To add a new example: drop a .yml/.yaml file in this directory, then add an
entry here. Guard the `id` in `WORKFLOW_EXAMPLE_IDS` below.

misc.label: WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_STATS_BAR_SETTING_ID
description: Feature flag ID for enabling / disabling the workflow execution stats bar UI

misc.label: WORKFLOW_INDEX_NAME
description: The main workflows index name.

misc.label: WORKFLOW_SYSTEM_INDEX_PREFIX
description:
Prefix of the workflows system indices.

The Kibana system user has the same permission on those indices than it has on Kibana system indices.

misc.label: WorkflowContext

objects.label: AlertEventPropsSchema
description: Alert-specific event properties. Only present when the workflow has an alert trigger.

objects.label: AlertEventSchema
description:
Full event schema (used for runtime validation of alert-triggered workflows).
For autocomplete, use getEventSchemaForTriggers() to get a trigger-aware schema.

objects.label: BaseEventSchema
description:
Base fields present on every trigger event (injected by the platform).
Custom trigger event schemas are merged on top of this for workflow context and autocomplete.
Timestamp is only present for event-driven (custom) triggers; see EventTimestampSchema.

objects.label: EventTimestampSchema
description: Timestamp injected by the platform for event-driven (custom) trigger events only.

objects.label: LIQUID_ALLOWED_TAGS
description:
LiquidJS tags supported in workflow templates.
Tags not in this set are removed from the engine.

objects.label: SystemConnectorsMap
description:
Map of regular (saved object) connector types -> their system connector equivalents.
Use this map to make the `connector-id` step config property optional for a given connector step type, allowing it to be executed via its linked system connector.
Pre-requisite for this to work:
- System connectors have empty config/secrets schemas. Make sure these system connectors are able to execute by receiving params alone.

objects.label: WORKFLOW_EXAMPLE_IDS
description: Allowlisted example IDs — prevents path traversal attacks when reading files

objects.label: WorkflowSchemaForAutocomplete
